tweets.title:
🚨REPORT: MAJOR ISI-BACKED TERROR MODULE BUSTED IN DELHI 
​📍 The Arrests: Delhi Police Special Cell has arrested 7 operatives: Anas (Anas Tyagi), Mohit (Yogi), Deepak Agrola (Deepak Ahrola), Arif, and 3 others.
📍 Masterminds: The network was directed by notorious gangster-turned-terrorists Shahzad Bhatti and Ajmal Gujjar.
📍 The Operation: This was a high-level ISI-backed terror-crime syndicate designed to destabilize the capital.
📍 Modus Operandi: Recruited youth via social media, using money and "underworld glamour" to lure them into illegal activities.
📍 Smuggling Routes: Massive illegal supply of arms, ammunition, and narcotics funneled from Pakistan into India via cross-border drone drops in Punjab.
📍 Seized Evidence: Recovered 5 semi-automatic pistols, 41 live cartridges, 7 mobile phones with encrypted chats, and an SUV.
📍 Targets: The module was actively conducting reconnaissance on religious sites and strategic installations in Delhi-NCR for imminent attacks.
📍 Financing: Used complex hawala channels and dead-drop delivery systems to hide their financial trail.
tweets.content:
🚨REPORT: MAJOR ISI-BACKED TERROR MODULE BUSTED IN DELHI
​📍 The Arrests: Delhi Police Special Cell has arrested 7 operatives: Anas (Anas Tyagi), Mohit (Yogi), Deepak Agrola (Deepak Ahrola), Arif, and 3 others.
📍 Masterminds: The network was directed by notorious gangster-turned-terrorists Shahzad Bhatti and Ajmal Gujjar.
📍 The Operation: This was a high-level ISI-backed terror-crime syndicate designed to destabilize the capital.
📍 Modus Operandi: Recruited youth via social media, using money and "underworld glamour" to lure them into illegal activities.
📍 Smuggling Routes: Massive illegal supply of arms, ammunition, and narcotics funneled from Pakistan into India via cross-border drone drops in Punjab.
📍 Seized Evidence: Recovered 5 semi-automatic pistols, 41 live cartridges, 7 mobile phones with encrypted chats, and an SUV.
📍 Targets: The module was actively conducting reconnaissance on religious sites and strategic installations in Delhi-NCR for imminent attacks.
📍 Financing: Used complex hawala channels and dead-drop delivery systems to hide their financial trail.

tweets.title:
RT by @OsintTV: On this day, 16 June, in different years, Jammu & Kashmir Police lost some of its bravest sons in terrorist attacks.

Shaurya Chakra awardee Inspector Arshad Khan; SI Feroz Ahmad Dar, Ct Shariq Ahmad Lone, Ct Tasveer Ahmad Dar, Ct Sheeraz Ahmad Bhat, Ct Sabzar Ahmad, SPO Mohammad Asif, PSI Rameez Raza, Ct Arif Nazir Khan, Follower Mohammad Shafi, Follower Mohammad Juma, SPO Gulshan Ahmad, SPO Farooq Ahmad and SPO Mohammad Shabir made the supreme sacrifice in the line of duty.

Their courage, patriotism and devotion to the nation remain an enduring source of inspiration. Though years may pass, their sacrifice continues to strengthen our resolve against terrorism and in service of the motherland.

Forever remembered. Forever honoured.

🇮🇳 Jai Hind.

#NeverForget #JammuAndKashmirPolice #SupremeSacrifice
tweets.content:
On this day, 16 June, in different years, Jammu & Kashmir Police lost some of its bravest sons in terrorist attacks.

Shaurya Chakra awardee Inspector Arshad Khan; SI Feroz Ahmad Dar, Ct Shariq Ahmad Lone, Ct Tasveer Ahmad Dar, Ct Sheeraz Ahmad Bhat, Ct Sabzar Ahmad, SPO Mohammad Asif, PSI Rameez Raza, Ct Arif Nazir Khan, Follower Mohammad Shafi, Follower Mohammad Juma, SPO Gulshan Ahmad, SPO Farooq Ahmad and SPO Mohammad Shabir made the supreme sacrifice in the line of duty.

Their courage, patriotism and devotion to the nation remain an enduring source of inspiration. Though years may pass, their sacrifice continues to strengthen our resolve against terrorism and in service of the motherland.

Forever remembered. Forever honoured.

🇮🇳 Jai Hind.

#NeverForget #JammuAndKashmirPolice #SupremeSacrifice

tweets.title:
RT by @OsintTV: "भिवाड़ी से नूंह और देवबंद तक फैली जांच: मस्जिद में मौलवी बनकर रह रहा था संदिग्ध, भोपाल ATS की कार्रवाई में खुला कथित नेटवर्क; पाकिस्तान स्थित हैंडलर्स, टेलीग्राम-व्हाट्सएप कनेक्शन, विदेशी फंडिंग और कई राज्यों में फैले संपर्कों की जांच तेज"

A major multi-state investigation is unfolding after security agencies expanded their probe into an alleged network linked to Pakistan-based handlers. The latest development came with the detention of a suspect from Rajasthan's Bhiwadi region, whose name reportedly surfaced during the interrogation of individuals arrested earlier by the Madhya Pradesh ATS.

Investigators are examining a complex web of digital communications, including Telegram and WhatsApp groups, suspected foreign contacts, financial transactions, and possible links stretching across Rajasthan, Haryana, Madhya Pradesh, and Uttar Pradesh. Authorities are also analyzing seized mobile phones, chat records, PDF documents, and other electronic evidence as part of a broader forensic investigation.

According to officials, the case began gaining momentum after the arrest of a suspect in Bhopal, which led investigators to additional individuals and locations. The probe has since expanded to examine alleged connections with Pakistan-based handlers, possible radicalization activities, digital networking, and the role of encrypted communication platforms.

Multiple agencies, including ATS units, intelligence officials, and state police forces, are now working together to determine the full extent of the network. Investigators are also exploring the possibility of foreign funding channels and whether additional individuals across different states may have been involved.

As the investigation progresses, security agencies remain focused on uncovering the complete picture behind the alleged network and identifying any potential threats to national security. Further revelations are expected as forensic analysis and interrogations continue.

#NationalSecurity #ATS #CounterTerrorism #IndiaSecurity #Pakistan #Intelligence #Bhiwadi #Rajasthan #Haryana #Bhopal #MadhyaPradesh #Deoband #Investigation #BreakingNews #SecurityForces #CounterIntelligence #DigitalForensics #Telegram #WhatsApp #ForeignFunding #DefenceNews #StrategicAffairs #IndianSecurity #HomelandSecurity #TINNetwork #DefenceJournalistSahil #Breaking #IndiaFirst #SecurityAlert #NationalInterest
tweets.content:

tweets.title:
LCA Mk2 – At the prototyping phase, with the first prototype coupled and lead by HAL.

AMCA – Will be developed by a consortium (non-HAL).

How does scrapping one positively impact the other (timelines, etc.)? My brain isn’t braining.

If someone called you that it was not a good thing, they should just call you a bad student of defence aviation.
tweets.content:
LCA Mk2 – At the prototyping phase, with the first prototype coupled and lead by HAL.

AMCA – Will be developed by a consortium (non-HAL).

How does scrapping one positively impact the other (timelines, etc.)? My brain isn’t braining.

If someone called you that it was not a good thing, they should just call you a bad student of defence aviation.

Yusuf Unjhawala 🇮🇳 (@YusufDFI)

I was called a Dalal for foreign firms when in 2019 I said we must scrap LCA Mk2 and pursue AMCA with all our resources. Instead, over the years we’ve had more projects added.

— https://nitter.net/YusufDFI/status/2066753389527232847#m

The air-launched #LRLACM is expected to weigh 900–1,000 kg as it does not require the heavy launch booster used by ground and naval variants.

This could allow an IAF Su-30MKI to carry between 2-4 missiles, depending on the final integration and mission configuration.

tweets.title: Whoever believed France would ever buy Pinaka was delusional. In France, dependence on imports is often viewed as a strategic vulnerability rather than an advantage. The French defence establishment places enormous value on sovereign capability and strategic autonomy. If France was unwilling to fully depend on imported rocket artillery systems like HIMARS, it was never realistically going to purchase Pinaka as a long-term solution.
tweets.content: Whoever believed France would ever buy Pinaka was delusional. In France, dependence on imports is often viewed as a strategic vulnerability rather than an advantage. The French defence establishment places enormous value on sovereign capability and strategic autonomy. If France was unwilling to fully depend on imported rocket artillery systems like HIMARS, it was never realistically going to purchase Pinaka as a long-term solution.

tweets.title:
India Blocks Telegram Till June 22 Ahead Of NEET Re-Examination, NTA Says 'Inconvenience Regretted'

The govt has also directed Telegram to disable its message-editing feature in India until June 30.

Media Report
tweets.content:
India Blocks Telegram Till June 22 Ahead Of NEET Re-Examination, NTA Says 'Inconvenience Regretted'

The govt has also directed Telegram to disable its message-editing feature in India until June 30.

Media Report
tweets.author: @OsintTV

tweets.title:
The Government of India has ordered a temporary restriction on Telegram access across the country until June 22, 2026, following recommendations from the NTA ahead of the NEET (UG) 2026 re-examination.

The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology has also directed Telegram to disable its message-editing feature in India until June 30, citing concerns over the fabrication of fake "paper leak" evidence related to national examinations.
tweets.content:
The Government of India has ordered a temporary restriction on Telegram access across the country until June 22, 2026, following recommendations from the NTA ahead of the NEET (UG) 2026 re-examination.

The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology has also directed Telegram to disable its message-editing feature in India until June 30, citing concerns over the fabrication of fake "paper leak" evidence related to national examinations.

🇺🇸 A U.S. Air Force B-52H Stratofortress (60-061), assigned to the 412th Test Wing at Edwards AFB, reportedly crashed shortly after takeoff during a radar test mission.

The aircraft, modified to test the new AN/APQ-188 AESA radar, lost control and crashed back onto the runway. All eight personnel on board, including USAF members and civilian contractors, were reported killed.

tweets.title:
RT by @OsintTV: Another chilling visual of Pakistan Army’s brutal crackdown in Rawalakot, PoJK, has surfaced today. The footage is from June 7, when Pakistani forces killed 26 protesters in a single day while attempting to crush the ongoing mass uprising.

With internet services suspended since June 5, the video has emerged only now. The footage clearly shows indiscriminate firing by Pakistani security personnel as unarmed civilians scramble for safety, exposing the scale of Islamabad’s repression in Pakistan-occupied Jammu & Kashmir.

tweets.title:
The system shall achieve a minimum speed of 180 m/s, operate at ranges exceeding 6 km, and provide an endurance of more than 30 seconds, including an IR source endurance of over 20 seconds to ensure effective missile lock-on. 

It shall be capable of launch operations at altitudes of 4,200 m and above, sustain flight at 1,500 m AGL or higher, and maintain a rate of climb greater than 30 m/s. The platform shall support launch preparation within 15 minutes, offer multiple launch angles for varied flight profiles, and incorporate a modular, transportable launcher compatible with existing Army vehicles. 

Additionally, the system shall comply with DAP 2020 requirements by incorporating a minimum of 50% indigenous content.
tweets.content:
The system shall achieve a minimum speed of 180 m/s, operate at ranges exceeding 6 km, and provide an endurance of more than 30 seconds, including an IR source endurance of over 20 seconds to ensure effective missile lock-on.

It shall be capable of launch operations at altitudes of 4,200 m and above, sustain flight at 1,500 m AGL or higher, and maintain a rate of climb greater than 30 m/s. The platform shall support launch preparation within 15 minutes, offer multiple launch angles for varied flight profiles, and incorporate a modular, transportable launcher compatible with existing Army vehicles.

Additionally, the system shall comply with DAP 2020 requirements by incorporating a minimum of 50% indigenous content.

Alpha Defense™🇮🇳 (@alpha_defense)

tweets.title:
RT by @OsintTV: बीकानेर से दिल्ली तक हाई अलर्ट: 10 हिंदू मंदिरों को बम से उड़ाने की धमकी, RSS मुख्यालय भी निशाने पर, आखिर कौन है खालिस्तान नेशनल आर्मी? पाकिस्तान-अजरबैजान कनेक्शन, डिजिटल टेरर नेटवर्क और 7 राज्यों में कथित गतिविधियों ने बढ़ाई सुरक्षा एजेंसियों की चिंता

राजस्थान के बीकानेर से राष्ट्रीय राजधानी दिल्ली तक सुरक्षा एजेंसियां सतर्क: मंदिरों, सरकारी इमारतों और RSS कार्यालय को धमकी देने वाला खालिस्तान नेशनल आर्मी मॉड्यूल क्या है? पाकिस्तान, अजरबैजान और अंतरराष्ट्रीय नेटवर्क के कथित संबंधों की जांच तेज

A series of bomb threats targeting Hindu temples, government institutions, and key organizations across multiple Indian states has triggered a major security response. Investigators are examining the activities of a group calling itself the Khalistan National Army (KNA), which has allegedly claimed responsibility for threatening emails sent to religious sites, government offices, and critical infrastructure.

The development has drawn attention to the evolving nature of modern security threats, where digital communications, psychological warfare, and transnational extremist networks increasingly intersect. Reports have also highlighted alleged connections involving Pakistan-based elements and meetings attended by anti-India actors abroad, prompting further scrutiny by security agencies.

Rajasthan, particularly Bikaner and other strategically important border districts, has emerged as a significant focus in the broader security discussion due to its proximity to sensitive frontier regions and military infrastructure. From Delhi and Haryana to Gujarat, Punjab, Maharashtra, and Rajasthan, authorities are examining whether the pattern of threats represents isolated incidents or part of a wider coordinated campaign aimed at creating fear and instability.

As India confronts an increasingly complex security environment, intelligence agencies, cybersecurity experts, and law enforcement authorities continue to investigate the origins, methods, and objectives behind these threats. The story highlights how modern extremist networks may seek to leverage cyberspace, propaganda, and psychological pressure alongside traditional tactics in an attempt to influence public perception and challenge national security frameworks.

#India #NationalSecurity #Bikaner #Rajasthan #Delhi #Khalistan #KhalistanNationalArmy #CounterTerrorism #HomelandSecurity #IndianSecurityForces #Intelligence #CyberSecurity #DigitalThreats #BorderSecurity #Pakistan #SouthAsia #Geopolitics #StrategicAffairs #DefenceNews #SecurityAlert #TerrorThreat #RSS #TempleSecurity #IndianArmy #DefenceJournalistSahil #TINNetwork

UK intercepts India-bound oil tanker Smyrtos linked to Russia’s shadow fleet

“The unflagged Aframax tanker Smyrtos, carrying around 101,400 tonnes of Russian Urals crude from Ust-Luga Port to India’s Sikka Port, was intercepted by UK authorities in English waters during its voyage to India.”

tweets.title:
Like it or not, 4.5-generation fighters will remain the workhorses of air forces throughout the next decade. Manned-Unmanned Teaming (MUM-T) combined with 5th-generation platforms will certainly offer greater effectiveness, but that does not make 4.5-generation aircraft obsolete, they will be effective with loyal wingman.

The real competition is increasingly shifting towards sensors, networking, electronic warfare, data fusion, and weapon guidance. 

In nutshell - A fighter’s combat value will depend as much on what it can see, share, and shoot as on its airframe generation or probably more.
tweets.content:
Like it or not, 4.5-generation fighters will remain the workhorses of air forces throughout the next decade. Manned-Unmanned Teaming (MUM-T) combined with 5th-generation platforms will certainly offer greater effectiveness, but that does not make 4.5-generation aircraft obsolete, they will be effective with loyal wingman.

The real competition is increasingly shifting towards sensors, networking, electronic warfare, data fusion, and weapon guidance.

In nutshell - A fighter’s combat value will depend as much on what it can see, share, and shoot as on its airframe generation or probably more.

tweets.title:
We must look at this from another angle. When @VishnuNDTV says that Tejas Mk1A/Mk2 could be “obsolete on arrival,” a few important questions naturally arise:

What timeframe is he referring to?
Against which specific technologies or threats is this assessment being made?
Does he hold a similar view about Rafale F4 and other contemporary 4.5-generation fighters?
Is this assessment based on inputs from the IAF, simulations, operational analysis, or war-gaming outcomes?

We should keep the discussion respectful and focus on asking meaningful questions. That is how a healthy debate develops, where different perspectives are examined, assumptions are challenged, and everyone learns something new.
tweets.content:
We must look at this from another angle. When @VishnuNDTV says that Tejas Mk1A/Mk2 could be “obsolete on arrival,” a few important questions naturally arise:

What timeframe is he referring to?
Against which specific technologies or threats is this assessment being made?
Does he hold a similar view about Rafale F4 and other contemporary 4.5-generation fighters?
Is this assessment based on inputs from the IAF, simulations, operational analysis, or war-gaming outcomes?

We should keep the discussion respectful and focus on asking meaningful questions. That is how a healthy debate develops, where different perspectives are examined, assumptions are challenged, and everyone learns something new.

Raflanker 🇮🇳 (@raflanker)

I dont know what @VishnuNDTV sir knows…
But the comment was unwarranted or was it out of outright frustration due to delays?
And if he is saying Tejas obsolete, as a senior journalist he atleast give an informed view to support it, which is expected from such senior journalist.

— https://nitter.net/raflanker/status/2066395776453161278#m

tweets.title:
Pakistan Air Force training aircraft crashes near Mardan; two pilots 72'd

Pakistan Air Force pilot Lieutenant Muhammad Qasim Abdullah and Pakistan Navy Lieutenant Taha Abbasi were 72'd in the crash.

A board constituted by Air Headquarter has initiated an investigation into the incident.
tweets.content:
Pakistan Air Force training aircraft crashes near Mardan; two pilots 72'd

Pakistan Air Force pilot Lieutenant Muhammad Qasim Abdullah and Pakistan Navy Lieutenant Taha Abbasi were 72'd in the crash.

A board constituted by Air Headquarter has initiated an investigation into the incident.
